articles.title: President Trump Wants to Reopen Alcatraz Prison
articles.summary: President Donald Trump is asking for $152 million to reopen Alcatraz, a famous old prison near San Francisco. Alcatraz closed in 1963 and is now a popular tourist spot run by the National Park Service, making money from visitors. Trump wants to turn it back into a modern, secure prison for very dangerous criminals. Some politicians, like Nancy Pelosi, think this is a bad idea because it would cost too much money and lose the tourism income. They say it's hard to run a prison on an island without running water, and all supplies must come by boat. Congress needs to approve the money for this plan to happen.

articles.title: Police Officers Leave Bag of Weapons on Street
articles.summary: In London, five police officers were taken off their regular jobs because they left a bag full of weapons on a street. A person walking by found the bag and called the police. Inside, there were guns, a Taser, and bullets. The police came quickly and took the bag away safely. They are now checking what happened to make sure it doesn't happen again. The bag was found near the home of London's mayor, Sadiq Khan, but it's not clear why it was there. This incident is serious because weapons should always be kept safe by the police.

articles.title: Astronauts Take Amazing Picture of Earth on Their Way to the Moon
articles.summary: NASA astronauts on the Artemis II mission are halfway to the Moon and have taken a beautiful photo of Earth. The crew, led by Commander Reid Wiseman, used a camera on their Orion spacecraft to capture the image. They were very happy when they reached this point in their journey. The picture shows Earth with blue oceans, a glowing atmosphere, and green lights at the poles. It looks upside down, with parts of Africa and South America visible. The astronauts are traveling to the Moon for the first time since 1972. They will go around the far side of the Moon and come back to Earth in April, landing in the Pacific Ocean. On the way, they enjoyed looking out the windows and taking more photos, even though the windows got dirty from their excitement.

articles.title: President Trump's Plan for College Sports
articles.summary: President Donald Trump signed an order to help college sports. He wants to make sure schools follow rules about players and money. If schools do not follow the rules, they might lose federal funding. This funding is money from the government that helps schools pay for things. The order talks about eligibility rules, which decide who can play, and transfers, when players move to different schools. College sports have become a big business with players earning millions. Trump asked Congress to act quickly, but it has been stuck for over a year. The NCAA and other groups are involved in making decisions. This order is Trump's second try to fix college sports.

articles.title: When Can People Get Their Pension Money?
articles.summary: The age when people can start getting their state pension is changing. Right now, it is 66 years old, but it is starting to go up to 67. This means some people born in 1960 will have to wait a little longer to get their money. The government is doing this because people are living longer, so they need to save money. The pension payments are also increasing by 4.8% soon, which means people will get a bit more money each month. To get the full pension, you usually need to have worked and paid national insurance for 35 years. Some younger people think the age might go up even more in the future, which worries them about their plans for travel and fun when they are older.

articles.title: How U.S. Forces Save Downed Air Crews
articles.summary: This article explains how U.S. military teams work to find and rescue pilots whose planes have crashed in combat. It features an interview with retired Master Sergeant Wes Bryant, who has experience in Air Force special operations. He describes that search and rescue missions have two main parts. First, they use all available resources like planes, intelligence, and local helpers to locate the missing crew member. Second, they protect the rescue planes from enemy air defenses, as Iran still has strong defenses despite claims of U.S. air superiority. The goal is to safely bring the crew back home.
